items.pregunta: Do I need prior programming experience?
items.respuesta: Basic knowledge helps but isn't strictly required. AI TechProducts is for people who want to build — you'll learn fundamentals while creating real products. What matters is the will to understand, not just generate.

items.pregunta: How long is the AI TechProducts program?
items.respuesta: Each cycle lasts 3 months, split into 3–4 week sprints. You'll ship at least 3 AI products, with weekly office hours for feedback and guidance.

items.pregunta: Can I use any AI tool?
items.respuesta: Yes! Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Lovable, no-code — use what helps you ship. The difference is you must understand and explain what the tools produced. We embrace tools; we reject blind dependency.

items.pregunta: What makes AI TechProducts different from other bootcamps?
items.respuesta: Traditional bootcamps teach you to follow tutorials. AI TechProducts requires real products, real users, and proof that you understand the fundamentals. Your portfolio is live products, not homework.

items.pregunta: How does the Umbral platform work?
items.respuesta: Umbral is your control center — shipped projects, fundamentals you've mastered, iteration history, and learning notes. Think of it as a living portfolio that proves you built with understanding.
Yes. We're committed to broadening access. Scholarship details for the next cohort will be announced when applications open. Follow us on social for updates.

items.pregunta: Are events in person or virtual?
items.respuesta: Both! Hackathons are often in person (like AI Hackathon Peru at UPCH), while bootcamps and AI TechProducts include virtual components to reach all of Latin America.
